<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The user wants to use the icon-16.png as the logo in the UI. Looking at the HTML, currently the onboarding view uses an SVG lightning bolt icon as the logo mark. 

The icon file is at `extension/icons/icon-16.png` but the glob shows only `.gitkeep` in the icons folder. However, the image file is shown in the recently viewed files as `d:\PRODUCT\PRODUCT\StyleSwift\extension\icons\icon-16.png`. Let me check the manifest to see what icons are referenced.

The user wants to replace the SVG logo mark in the onboarding view with the actual icon image. I need to:


1. In the onboarding view, replace the SVG logo-mark with an `<img>` tag pointing to the icon
2. In the topbar (main view), add the logo/icon

Let me check the manifest to understand the icon paths.</details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Now I understand the structure. The icon is at `icons/icon-16.png` relative to the extension root, while the sidepanel HTML is at `sidepanel/index.html`. So the relative path from the HTML would be `../icons/icon-16.png`.

I need to:
1. Replace the SVG logo-mark in the onboarding view with an `<img>` using the icon
2. Possibly also add the logo to the topbar in the main view

Let me update the HTML to:
1. In the onboarding `logo-mark` div, replace the SVG with an `<img>` tag


2. In the topbar, add the logo icon next to or as part of the header

Replacing the SVG with an image element...

I'm noticing the icon-16.png might be too small at larger display sizes, so I should consider using icon-48.png or icon-128.png instead for better clarity in the onboarding view, while keeping it smaller in the topbar.</details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The JS code looks for a `.dot` element inside `#status-dot`. Since I've replaced the `status-indicator` div (which contained a `.dot` span) with just the logo `<img>`, the `querySelector(".dot")` will return `null` and the function will early return at line 661. 

However, the style-badge is also appended to `DOM.statusDot` which is now the img. That's fine as it will still append the badge next to the logo. But the status color changes via `.dot` class won't apply anymore which is the desired behavior.

Actually, let me think about this more carefully. The user wants to replace the status dot with the logo. 

Since the user specified "用logo图标替代" (just replace with the logo icon), I'll keep the code as-is—the `querySelector(".dot")` will return null and skip the status color updates, while the style-badge will still append to the img element's parent area.

Wait, but appending a child to an img element won't work since img is a void element. I should wrap the img in a div container instead. Actually, the cleanest solution is to keep the existing `status-indicator` div as the `#status-dot` container and just swap out the dot span for the logo image inside it—that way all the badge logic continues to work without needing structural changes.</details></think>

---

完成！以下是修改内容：

**HTML**：`#status-dot` 容器内只保留 logo 图片，去掉了原来的 `.dot` span。

**JS**：两处状态函数（`applyTopBarState` 和 `updateStatusIndicator`）都从查找 `.dot` 改为查找 `.topbar-logo`，状态 class 依然添加在该元素上。

**CSS**：
- 去掉了 `.dot` 的圆点颜色样式
- 改为通过 `opacity` 和 `filter` 在 logo 图片上体现状态：
  - `ready`：正常显示
  - `processing`：轻柔的呼吸动画（亮度/透明度循环）
  - `error`：轻微灰度 + 变暗
  - `restricted`：半透明 + 完全灰度

---

```
  // ������ʽ�ձ�
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="run_terminal_command_v2">
<details><summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: 
$file = "d:\PRODUCT\PRODUCT\StyleSwift\extension\sidepanel\panel.js"
$lines = Get-Content $file -Encoding UTF8
# 行号从0开始索引，第680-692行对应索引679-691

```bash

$file = "d:\PRODUCT\PRODUCT\StyleSwift\extension\sidepanel\panel.js"
```

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The scripts are all `type="module"`, so `export` should be valid. The error "Uncaught SyntaxError: Unexpected token 'export'" might be something else. Let me check the exact line 6192 and surrounding content again more carefully.

Actually, wait - this might be because our PowerShell edit changed the file encoding. When PowerShell reads and writes the file, it might have changed the encoding from UTF-8 BOM to something else, or the other way around. Let me check the actual encoding.

Or maybe the issue is that the edit removed lines but introduced a line numbering mismatch. 

I'm wondering if the PowerShell operation altered the file's BOM or encoding, since that could cause the module to not be recognized properly even with `type="module"` declared. The "Unexpected token 'export'" error typically means the file isn't being treated as a module, so I should verify the file encoding first. The UTF-8 BOM from PowerShell's Set-Content might be causing parser issues, though modern browsers should handle it fine. Let me look more carefully at what actually changed around the deletion point—maybe the file got corrupted during the edit in a way that's breaking the syntax near line 6192.</details></think>

---

模块脚本中 `export` 是合法的，但 PowerShell 编辑文件时可能改变了文件编码（加了 BOM）导致解析异常。先检查删除点附近的代码是否完整：

---

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="read_file_v2">
<details><summary>Tool use: **read_file_v2** • Read file: d:\PRODUCT\PRODUCT\StyleSwift\extension\sidepanel\panel.js</summary>

</details>
</tool-use>

---

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The code around the deletion point looks fine syntactically. The issue is likely the file encoding change by PowerShell. Let me check the beginning of the file for BOM characters.</details></think>

---

代码结构完整。问题在于 PowerShell `Set-Content -Encoding UTF8` 会写入 UTF-8 BOM，导致浏览器解析模块时出错。用 PowerShell 重新保存，去掉 BOM：

---

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="run_terminal_command_v2">
<details><summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: 
$file = "d:\PRODUCT\PRODUCT\StyleSwift\extension\sidepanel\panel.js"
$content = Get-Content $file -Raw -Encoding UTF8
# 去掉 BOM（如果存在）
if ($content.StartsWith([char]0xFEFF)) {
    $content = $content.Substring(1)
    Write-Host "BOM removed"
} else {
    Write-Host "No BOM found"
}
# 用无 BOM 的 UTF8 写回
[System.IO.File]::WriteAllText($file, $content, (New-Object System.Text.UTF8Encoding $false))
Write-Host "Saved without BOM"
</summary>

```bash

$file = "d:\PRODUCT\PRODUCT\StyleSwift\extension\sidepanel\panel.js"
$content = Get-Content $file -Raw -Encoding UTF8
# 去掉 BOM（如果存在）
if ($content.StartsWith([char]0xFEFF)) {
    $content = $content.Substring(1)
    Write-Host "BOM removed"
} else {
    Write-Host "No BOM found"
}
# 用无 BOM 的 UTF8 写回
[System.IO.File]::WriteAllText($file, $content, (New-Object System.Text.UTF8Encoding $false))
Write-Host "Saved without BOM"

```

```
BOM removed
Saved without BOM

```
</details>
</tool-use>

---

确认 panel.css 也有同样问题并修复：

---

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="run_terminal_command_v2">
<details><summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: 
foreach ($f in @(
    "d:\PRODUCT\PRODUCT\StyleSwift\extension\sidepanel\panel.css"
)) {
    $content = Get-Content $f -Raw -Encoding UTF8
    if ($content.StartsWith([char]0xFEFF)) {
        $content = $content.Substring(1)
        [System.IO.File]::WriteAllText($f, $content, (New-Object System.Text.UTF8Encoding $false))
        Write-Host "$f - BOM removed"
    } else {
        Write-Host "$f - No BOM"
    }
}
</summary>

```bash

foreach ($f in @(
    "d:\PRODUCT\PRODUCT\StyleSwift\extension\sidepanel\panel.css"
)) {
    $content = Get-Content $f -Raw -Encoding UTF8
    if ($content.StartsWith([char]0xFEFF)) {
        $content = $content.Substring(1)
        [System.IO.File]::WriteAllText($f, $content, (New-Object System.Text.UTF8Encoding $false))
        Write-Host "$f - BOM removed"
    } else {
        Write-Host "$f - No BOM"
    }
}

```

```
d:\PRODUCT\PRODUCT\StyleSwift\extension\sidepanel\panel.css - BOM removed

```
</details>
</tool-use>

---

两个文件的 UTF-8 BOM 都已去除。问题原因：PowerShell 5.x 的 `Set-Content -Encoding UTF8` 默认会写入带 BOM 的 UTF-8，浏览器在解析 ES 模块时遇到文件开头的 BOM 字节导致 `export` 关键字识别失败。现在重新加载扩展应该恢复正常。
